name: Safe-RLHF
tagline: Train LLMs that stay helpful and harmless with separate reward and cost signals
metaDescription: Safe-RLHF (Beaver) is an open-source RLHF framework from PKU that splits helpfulness and safety signals to train constrained, value-aligned LLMs.
overview: Safe-RLHF (codenamed Beaver) is an open-source RLHF framework from the PKU-Alignment team at Peking University. It covers the full alignment pipeline for popular pre-trained models like LLaMA, OPT, and Baichuan: supervised fine-tuning (SFT), reward and cost model training, standard RLHF, and the team's Safe RLHF method.

What sets it apart is how it handles safety. Instead of folding everything into a single reward, it trains a separate reward model for helpfulness and a separate cost model for harm, then optimizes the policy to maximize reward while keeping cost under a constraint. The result is a model that aims to be both helpful and harmless rather than trading one for the other.

It is built for alignment researchers and ML engineers who want a reproducible pipeline. The project also ships a large human-labeled preference dataset (PKU-SafeRLHF) and pre-trained Beaver-7B, reward, and cost checkpoints, so you can reproduce results or start from existing weights.
features: Supports the full pipeline: SFT, reward and cost model training, RLHF, and Safe RLHF, all in one framework
Separates helpfulness (reward) from safety (cost) so the policy is optimized under an explicit safety constraint
Works with popular pre-trained models including LLaMA, OPT, and Baichuan
Ships the PKU-SafeRLHF human-labeled preference dataset (up to 1M pairs of helpful and harmless preferences)
Provides pre-trained Beaver-7B, reward, and cost checkpoints on Hugging Face
Built on DeepSpeed, with multi-scale safety evaluation via BIG-bench and GPT-4 evaluation

gettingStarted.intro: Safe-RLHF is installed from source via a conda environment, then run as a sequence of training scripts ending with an interactive CLI demo. The commands below come straight from the project's README.

steps.heading: Clone and create the environment
steps.body: Clone the repository and build the conda environment from the provided recipe file.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/PKU-Alignment/safe-rlhf.git
cd safe-rlhf
conda env create --file conda-recipe.yaml

steps.heading: Run supervised fine-tuning
steps.body: Start the pipeline by fine-tuning a base model. Point it at any Hugging Face model name or local checkpoint.

steps.heading: Train reward and cost models, then run Safe RLHF
steps.body: Train the helpfulness reward model and the safety cost model from the SFT checkpoint, then optimize the policy under the safety constraint with PPO-Lagrangian.
steps.code:
bash scripts/reward-model.sh --model_name_or_path output/sft --output_dir output/rm
bash scripts/cost-model.sh --model_name_or_path output/sft --output_dir output/cm
bash scripts/ppo-lag.sh \
    --actor_model_name_or_path output/sft \
    --reward_model_name_or_path output/rm \
    --cost_model_name_or_path output/cm \
    --output_dir output/ppo-lag

steps.heading: Chat with the trained model
steps.body: Launch the interactive CLI demo to test your model.

useCases: Reproducing constrained alignment research where safety must be enforced as a separate constraint, not blended into one reward
Fine-tuning an open base model (LLaMA, OPT, Baichuan) into a helpful and harmless chat assistant
Training and evaluating reward and cost models on human preference data for safety-aware ranking
Benchmarking a model's safety with the included BIG-bench and GPT-4 evaluation tooling
